[IMPLEMENT A GAUSSIAN NAIVE BAYES ALGORITHM ON SCIKIT-LEARN'S LOAD_DIGITS DATASET INCLUDING CROSS-VALIDATION AND HYPERPARAMETER TUNING]  
[COMPARE ITS PERFORMANCE AGAINST A MULTINOMIAL NAIVE BAYES MODEL]  
[THE CORE TASK IS TO DECOMPOSE THE SYSTEM PROMPT INTO FOUR INDEPENDENT MODULES]  
[THE FIRST MODULE IDENTIFIES THE CORE OBJECTIVE OF THE ORIGINAL PROMPT]  
[THE SECOND MODULE ANALYZES THE ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION TO EXTRACT KEY ELEMENTS SUCH AS THE ROLE, CONSTRAINTS, AND OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS]  
[THE THIRD MODULE ASSIGN EACH OF THE FOUR ASPECTS—ROLE DEFINITION, CONSTRAINTS, OUTPUT FORMAT, AND ERROR PREVENTION—to A SEPARATE MODULE FOR CLARITY AND INDEPENDENCE]  
[THE FOURTH MODULE ENURES THAT ALL ASPECTS ARE ADDRESSED AND THAT THE FINAL RESPONSE MEETS ALL CONSTRAINTS, INCLUDING THE WORD COUNT, STARTING/ENDING WORD, AND KEYWORD LIMITS]  
[THE RESPONSE MUST BE IN ENGLISH, UNDER 36 SENTENCES, AND START AND END WITH THE SAME WORD]  
[THE WORD CURRENT SHOULD APPEAR 2 TIMES]  
[THE FINAL WORD IS TASK]
